content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


February 8, 2001




Lori J. Barker, Assistant City Attorney
Office of the City Attorney
City of Chico
411 Main Street
Post Office Box 3420
Chico, CA 95927

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. I-00-265

Dear Ms. Barker:

       This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act of the ("Act").¹ Specifically, you inquire as to the reporting
obligations of newly elected Councilman Larry Wahl.

FACTS

       Councilman Larry Wahl's spouse is in a partnership with her brother. The
partnership owns five pieces of property. One of those properties is located in the City
and is leased to the United States Postal Service. The other four properties are located
outside of the City limits and also produce rental income for the partnership. The
partnership's income and expenses for that year are calculated and a distribution is made
to the partners in the amount of the net income at the end of each year. Rental income
from the five properties is the only income which is received by this partnership.

       Councilmember Wahl's spouse was in the partnership prior to their marriage, and
all of the income which she receives is kept by her as separate property and is not commingled with the community property.

_____________
       ¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. I-00-265
Page No. 2

Councilmember Wahl's spouse and her brother are also co-owners of a business
which is located outside the City limits. It is anticipated that Mrs. Wahl's brother will be
buying out her interest in this business in the future. Her brother lives in the City of
Chico.

ANALYSIS

Spouse's Investments

Under the Act's conflict of interest provisions, public officials at all levels of state
and local government must file periodic statements disclosing certain personal economic
interests that may be affected during the exercise of their official duties. As an elected
city council person, Mr. Wahl is subject to the broad disclosure requirements of section
87200.

Moreover, section 87202 requires a newly elected official specified in section
87200 to file a statement of economic interests disclosing, among other things "his or her
investments ... held on the date of assuming office."

"'Investment' means any financial interest in or security
issued by a business entity, including but not limited to
common stock, preferred stock, rights, warrants, options,
debt instruments and any partnership or other ownership
interest owned directly, indirectly or beneficially by the
public official, or other filer, or his or her immediate
family, if the business entity or any parent, subsidiary or
otherwise related business entity has an interest in real
property in the jurisdiction, or does business or plans to do
business in the jurisdiction, or has done business within the
jurisdiction at any time during the two years prior to the
time any statement or other action is required under this
title. No asset shall be deemed an investment unless its fair
market value equals or exceeds one thousand dollars
($1,000).... Investments of an individual includes a pro rata
share of investments of any business entity, mutual fund, or
trust in which the individual or immediate family owns,
directly, indirectly or beneficially, a 10-percent interest or
greater."

Section 82034.
(Emphasis added.)

An official's "immediate family" includes his or her spouse and dependent
children. (Section 82029.)

File No. I-00-265
Page No. 3

The Act provides no exception from this disclosure requirement for assets held by
a spouse which are the spouse's separate property or for those which were acquired prior
to the marriage. Therefore, on his assuming office Statement of Economic Interests, Mr.
Wahl must disclose each investment held by his spouse in a business entity which is
located in or doing business in the District's jurisdiction, if the fair market value of the
investment was $1,000 or more on the date he assumed office.²

SEI Schedules

In completing his Statement of Economic Interests, Mr. Wahl shall report on
Schedule A-2:

• The investments of his wife's partnership comprised of the property located within
the jurisdiction and two miles outside thereof, as described above. (§§ 87206,
82035.)
• The lessees of the other properties, which are outside of the City, if they also do
business within the City. (§§ 87202, 87207(b)(2).)
• His community property share in any income she receives from the partnership,
unless his wife's salary is her separate property. (§ 87207.)

The properties listed on Schedule A-2 owned by the partnership need not also be listed
on Schedule B.

Spouse's Business Interest

Your letter does not indicate the nature of the business owned by Mr. Wahl's
spouse and her brother, which is located outside the jurisdiction. Assuming the business
does not have interests in real property or investments (otherwise, see discussion above),
an official is not required to disclose a spouse's income if that income is treated as the
spouse's separate property. To be considered separate property income, the income must
be maintained in separate accounts; the separate funds may not be commingled with
community funds or used to pay for community expenses; and community efforts must
not be used to produce or enhance the separate income of either spouse. (See Katz
Advice Letter, A-86-335.)

___________________________
² The jurisdiction of a local government agency is the region, county, city, district, or other
geographical area in which it has jurisdiction. (Section 82035.)

File No. I-00-265
Page No. 4

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Acting General Counsel

By: C. Scott Tocher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question: Your inquiry concerns the reporting obligations of newly elected Councilman Larry Wahl regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act, specifically with respect to investments and business interests held by his spouse.
sections.conclusion: On his assuming office Statement of Economic Interests, Mr. Wahl must disclose each investment held by his spouse in a business entity which is located in or doing business in the jurisdiction, if the fair market value of the investment was $1,000 or more on the date he assumed office. The Act provides no exception from this disclosure requirement for assets held by a spouse which are the spouse's separate property or for those which were acquired prior to the marriage.

sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act for a newly elected city councilman regarding his spouse's partnership interests in rental properties and co-ownership in a business, where the spouse's income is maintained as separate property?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Councilman Wahl must disclose his spouse's partnership interests in properties within and near the city on Schedule A-2 of his Statement of Economic Interests, regardless of whether such assets are the spouse's separate property. For the outside business interest, disclosure is not required if it has no real property interests, does not do business in the jurisdiction, and the income is properly maintained as separate property.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; LLM: Document has OCR quality issues but clear structure with FACTS, ANALYSIS, and conclusions sections. File number is I-00-265 (letter dated February 8, 2001). Minor OCR errors in headers and text (e.g., 'Farn Pouucel' for 'Fair Political'). Question synthesized from opening paragraphs as no explicit 'QUESTION' header present.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your inquiry concerns the reporting obligations of newly elected Councilman Larry Wahl regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act, specifically with respect to investments and business interests held by his spouse.

CONCLUSION: On his assuming office Statement of Economic Interests, Mr. Wahl must disclose each investment held by his spouse in a business entity which is located in or doing business in the jurisdiction, if the fair market value of the investment was $1,000 or more on the date he assumed office. The Act provides no exception from this disclosure requirement for assets held by a spouse which are the spouse's separate property or for those which were acquired prior to the marriage.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that a newly elected city councilman must disclose his spouse's investment interests in a partnership owning rental properties in the jurisdiction on his Statement of Economic Interests, even though the assets are the spouse's separate property. The disclosure requirement applies broadly under the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions.
